commands.review_summary:
The PR removes recursive display-change recovery retries from `MenuBarVisibilityWatcher`, adds a synchronous post-recreation macOS 26 guidance check, and adds focused visibility/recreation tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level triage: current main still schedules repeated display-change recovery while snapshots remain blocked. A full live reproduction still requires macOS 26 plus a menu-bar manager and a display configuration change.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer review of the compatibility tradeoff and optional live bundle validation.

target.title: fix: stop retrying display-change recovery to avoid corrupting menu bar managers
target.body: ## Summary - Addresses #1175 - Remove the recursive `schedulePostScreenChangeRecoveryVerification` retry loop from `verifyScreenChangeRecoveryIfNeeded` - Preserve the macOS 26 Allow-in-Menu-Bar guidance via a synchronous re-check immediately after the single recreation ## Problem The retry loop introduced in c305858c called `recreateStatusItemsForVisibilityRecovery()` up to four times per display event. After each recreation, a menu bar manager (Bartender, Ice, Hidden Bar) can temporarily leave the new `NSStatusItem` without a window while re-registering it — making it appear blocked and triggering another recreation cycle. Each cycle is visible to Control Center and menu bar managers as a brand-new item, corrupting Control Center layout and causing managers to re-expose the previously hidden icon. ## Why a synchronous re-check is safe for guidance AppKit materialises the status item button and window synchronously before returning from `recreateStatusItemsForVisibilityRecovery`. Only a system-level block (e.g. the macOS Tahoe allow-list) leaves the item blocked at that point — which is exactly the case where the guidance dialog is useful. A menu bar manager's transient re-registration state resolves in milliseconds and does not survive the synchronous boundary. ## Test plan - [ ] Run `./Scripts/compile_and_run.sh` - [ ] Hide CodexBar via Ice or another menu bar manager - [ ] Trigger a display change (System Settings → Displays → change resolution and revert) - [ ] Confirm Control Center is not corrupted and the manager does not re-expose CodexBar's icon - [ ] Confirm `"recreating status items again"` appears at most once per display event: ``` log stream --process CodexBar --level debug | grep -i "recreating status items" ``` Screenshots: After: Before:
target.author: diazdesandi
